compiled_state.title: Eliminate remaining 3+ level Skill tool nesting in sprint workflow
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context
When /dso:sprint invokes /dso:preplanning via Skill tool (level 2), preplanning currently invokes /dso:design-wireframe via Skill tool (level 3), which can further invoke /dso:ui-discover (level 4). At 3+ nesting levels, control fails to return to the sprint orchestrator. Design-wireframe is too complex (868 lines, 6 phases) to inline as a read, so the fix is architectural: convert it from a Skill invocation into a dedicated sub-agent dispatched by preplanning, capping design work at 1 level below the orchestrator.

## Success Criteria
1. The sprint to preplanning to design-wireframe nesting chain (3 levels) is eliminated; design work runs at most 1 level below the orchestrator
2. The design-wireframe to ui-discover nesting chain is eliminated; ui-discover runs at orchestrator level when needed
3. A new dso:ui-designer sub-agent (agent definition in plugins/dso/agents/) handles design creation (current Phases 1-4, 6), returning structured results to the orchestrator
4. When the ui-designer agent detects a missing/stale UI discovery cache, it returns CACHE_MISSING with instructions; the orchestrator (preplanning) runs /dso:ui-discover and re-dispatches with a cap (max 2 CACHE_MISSING cycles before user escalation) to prevent unbounded loops
5. Design review (current Phase 5) is orchestrator-managed: preplanning runs review-protocol on design artifacts and loops with feedback (max 3 cycles) before escalation; escalation emits INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED in non-interactive sessions rather than blocking on AskUserQuestion
6. Scope-split proposals from the ui-designer agent bubble up in the return payload; preplanning applies a precedence rule: its own Foundation/Enhancement split is authoritative, and agent scope-split proposals are only evaluated when preplanning has not already split the story

## Approach
Convert /dso:design-wireframe from a Skill into a dedicated dso:ui-designer sub-agent dispatched by preplanning. Extract design creation logic (Phases 1-4, 6) into the agent definition, preserving Figma config-gated SVG requirements (design.figma_collaboration=true). Move design review (Phase 5) up to preplanning as an orchestrator-managed loop. The agent returns structured results including design artifacts, scope-split proposals, and cache status via worktree-safe relative paths. Preplanning handles cache generation, review loops, retry/escalation, and atomic deployment of the agent definition alongside agent-routing.conf. The design-wireframe skill file becomes a redirect stub.

## Dependencies
- 2f7a-7770: Migrate browser automation to @playwright/cli (closed)
- 50fa-ef59: Skill Nesting Observability trace logging (closed)

comments.body:
Remaining 3+ level Skill nesting scenarios to fix:

1. sprint → preplanning → design-wireframe (3 levels): preplanning Step 6 invokes /dso:design-wireframe via Skill tool. Fix: inline design-wireframe execution or restructure preplanning to return UI story candidates for sprint to invoke.

2. sprint → preplanning → design-wireframe → ui-discover (4 levels): design-wireframe Step 4 conditionally invokes /dso:ui-discover. Fix: inline ui-discover or pre-run before design-wireframe.

Related bug: 335b-c846 (fixed scenarios 2 and 3 in that investigation).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context

When /dso:sprint invokes /dso:preplanning via Skill tool (level 2), and preplanning invokes /dso:design-wireframe via Skill tool (level 3), control fails to return to the sprint orchestrator. Design-wireframe can further invoke /dso:ui-discover (level 4). Bug 335b-c846 fixed analogous nesting for review-protocol by converting Skill calls to inline reads, but design-wireframe is too complex (868 lines, 6 phases) for that pattern. Neither design-wireframe nor ui-discover is user-invocable — both exist solely as sub-steps of preplanning.

## Success Criteria

1. The sprint → preplanning → design-wireframe nesting chain (3 levels) is eliminated; design work runs at most 1 level below the orchestrator
2. The design-wireframe → ui-discover nesting chain is eliminated; ui-discover runs at orchestrator level when needed
3. A new dso:ui-designer sub-agent (agent definition in plugins/dso/agents/) handles design creation (current Phases 1-4, 6), returning structured results to the orchestrator
4. When the ui-designer agent detects a missing/stale UI discovery cache, it returns CACHE_MISSING with instructions; the orchestrator (preplanning) runs /dso:ui-discover and re-dispatches
5. Design review (current Phase 5) is orchestrator-managed: preplanning runs review-protocol on design artifacts and loops with feedback (max 3 cycles) before escalation
6. Scope-split proposals from the ui-designer agent bubble up in the return payload; preplanning decides whether to act on them

## Scenario Analysis

- Agent returns CACHE_MISSING but orchestrator fails to handle → preplanning must have explicit error handling for this return status, not just the happy path
- Design review feedback loop creates large context in preplanning → cap at 3 cycles (matching current behavior) with explicit escalation to user
- Existing test coverage (test-skill-nesting-depth.sh) needs updating to validate the new agent-based pattern instead of Skill-based invocation
- The design-wireframe skill file becomes a redirect stub (like project-setup) → must update test-sub-agent-guard.sh and test-redirect-stubs.sh

## Dependencies

- 2f7a-7770: Migrate browser automation to @playwright/cli — ui-designer agent needs a reliable browser automation path

## Approach

Convert /dso:design-wireframe from a Skill into a dedicated dso:ui-designer sub-agent dispatched by preplanning. Extract design creation logic (Phases 1-4, 6) into the agent definition. Move design review (Phase 5) up to preplanning as an orchestrator-managed loop. The agent returns structured results including design artifacts, scope-split proposals, and cache status. Preplanning handles all cross-cutting concerns: cache generation, review loops, retry/escalation.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered — researched Playwright MCP access in sub-agents; found multiple open bugs (anthropics/claude-code#13605, #13898, #21560); discovered @playwright/cli as alternative (~4x fewer tokens, disk-based, no timeout issues)
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): not triggered
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Adversarial review: 9 findings, 5 accepted. Full exchange: /tmp/workflow-plugin-bce9dedff503c924/adversarial-review-28cb-a23a.json
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
### Planning Intelligence Log (re-run)

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none (all dependencies closed, no external integrations introduced)
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered — 9 surviving scenarios, 4 filtered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 9 (6 high, 3 medium)
  - Key findings accepted: CACHE_MISSING retry cap, scope-split precedence rule, SUB-AGENT-GUARD fallback path, atomic agent routing, Figma SVG config-gating, worktree-safe artifact paths
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: scenario analysis re-run 1 time (re-run requested to recover lost original adversarial review findings)
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keywords: agent definitions, skill files (bot-psychologist not re-dispatched; existing comments confirm original run covered this)
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
EPIC_COMPLETION_VERIFIER: NOT_VERIFIED

SC1 PASS: sprint→preplanning→design-wireframe nesting chain eliminated; design-wireframe is redirect stub; preplanning dispatches dso:ui-designer via Agent tool.
SC2 PASS: ui-designer returns CACHE_MISSING instead of invoking ui-discover internally; ui-discover runs at orchestrator level via dispatch-protocol.md Section 3.
SC3 PASS: plugins/dso/agents/ui-designer.md exists (595 lines, Lite+Full tracks, UI_DESIGNER_PAYLOAD); CLAUDE.md agent table updated.
SC4 PASS: CACHE_MISSING retry cap (2 attempts, 3 total) implemented in dispatch-protocol.md Section 3; INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED escalation present.
SC5 FAIL: Design review loop not implemented. dispatch-protocol.md Section 4 explicitly excludes Phase 5, only tagging design:pending_review. Bug ticket: f227-88bb.
SC6 FAIL: Scope-split precedence gate not enforced in dispatch-protocol.md Section 5. SKILL.md line 385 has behavioral note only. Bug ticket: 9678-b9a6.

Additional: implementation-plan/SKILL.md line 709 has stale /dso:design-wireframe reference. Bug ticket: c3f2-b72c.

Epic remains in_progress pending f227-88bb, 9678-b9a6 resolution.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
EPIC_COMPLETION_VERIFIER: VERIFIED

SC1 PASS: Sprint→preplanning→design-wireframe 3-level nesting eliminated; ui-designer runs as direct Agent tool dispatch from preplanning orchestrator (previously verified)
SC2 PASS: design-wireframe→ui-discover nesting eliminated; ui-discover runs at orchestrator level via Skill tool in Section 3 CACHE_MISSING retry loop (previously verified)
SC3 PASS: dso:ui-designer agent definition handles design creation phases 1-4/6 and returns structured UI_DESIGNER_PAYLOAD (previously verified)
SC4 PASS: CACHE_MISSING return triggers orchestrator-level ui-discover refresh with 2-retry cap before INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED escalation (previously verified)
SC5 PASS: Section 4 contains review loop with review_cycle_count=0/max_review_cycles=3 state vars, /dso:review-protocol invocation, REVIEW_PASS break branch, REVIEW_FAIL increment+re-dispatch branch, and INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED non-interactive escalation at max cycles
SC6 PASS: Section 5 opens with splitRole guard that checks for splitRole:Foundation/Enhancement; when present skips agent scope_split_proposals as authoritative preplanning split; when absent processes proposals normally

All success criteria met.
comments.author: Test
